title: BloombergGPT: A Large Language Model for Finance
corpus_composition: FinPile (Bloomberg-proprietary: web, news, filings, press releases, Bloomberg curated content) at ~363B tokens, mixed ~1:1 with The Pile + C4 + Wikipedia. Heavily US-centric.
input_representation: 2048 context window. Unigram tokenizer (~131k vocab) trained on a finance-tilted corpus. No special multimodal or numeric encoding.
backbone_family: Dense Transformer, BLOOM-style decoder-only with ALiBi positional encoding.
core_mechanism: Train a 50B dense Transformer from scratch on a curated finance-heavy corpus mixed ~1:1 with general web/curated text using standard causal LM. The architectural bet is null — no finance-specific architectural changes. The empirical bet is entirely on the corpus.
training_recipe: 64 nodes x 8 x A100-40GB; ~53 days wall time; cosine LR schedule with peak ~6e-5; batch ~2048 sequences; BLOOM-style training pipeline. No published continued-pretraining or finance-only ablation at the same scale.
key_empirical_claim: At 50B params, a finance-corpus-mixed model beats similarly-sized general open LLMs (GPT-NeoX 20B, OPT 66B) on finance-specific zero/few-shot tasks (FPB, FiQA SA, Headline, ConvFinQA) while remaining competitive on general BIG-bench / MMLU. Gain is largest on tasks with finance-specific entities and jargon.
benchmark_and_metrics: FPB (sentiment), FiQA SA, ConvFinQA (multi-step numerical reasoning), Headline (classification), Finance NER. General: BIG-bench, MMLU. Zero/few-shot.
ablation_or_evidence_notes: No same-scale general-only baseline (no '50B trained from scratch on Pile only'). No continued-pretraining of a strong open base for comparison. No contamination check on FPB/FiQA. Evaluations are mostly few-shot rather than fine-tuned.
foundation_model_potential: Demonstrates that with a modest training budget (~half a trillion tokens), a balanced finance/general corpus shifts downstream finance performance materially without architectural novelty. The corpus is the lever.
why_relevant_for_inhouse_training: Canonical reference for the from-scratch-on-finance-corpus path. Calibrates (a) the token scale needed for noticeable lift at 50B (~360B finance tokens) and (b) that a 1:1 finance/general mix is at least viable. Key open question for our in-house plan: whether a public-data analogue (EDGAR 10-K/10-Q/8-K + transcripts + finance news + regulatory) at similar scale yields comparable lift, given FinPile is proprietary and irreproducible.
main_limitations: Proprietary corpus blocks reproducibility. No continued-pretraining baseline. No comparison to a stronger general LLM (e.g., a contemporary 70B+) which is the most relevant baseline today. Few-shot eval only; no instruction-tuned variant released.
deployment_risks: Look-ahead leakage (proprietary FinPile temporal split is not auditable); base-model attribution confound (cannot isolate the 'finance corpus' effect from the 'from-scratch 50B budget' effect); eval contamination (FPB/FiQA are public — likely in pretraining data); regime mismatch (training cutoff means degradation on novel market structure).
implementation_takeaways: 1) Use ~1:1 finance/general as a starting mix rather than finance-only. 2) Build a FinPile-analogue from public sources: SEC EDGAR (10-K/10-Q/8-K), licensable transcripts, regulatory text, finance news. 3) Tokenizer need not be finance-specialized at 50B. 4) Establish a strict temporal holdout from day 1 — training data ends at year T-1, eval on year T.
must_test_hypotheses: H_bloom1: At <=13B, continued-pretraining a strong general open base (Llama-3-70B / 2026-vintage equivalent) on a FinPile-analogue matches or exceeds from-scratch 50B at a fraction of the budget. H_bloom2: The 1:1 finance/general mix is dominated by ratios closer to 1:9 once the general model is itself competitive. H_bloom3: Most BloombergGPT finance gain comes from FinPile coverage of niche entities and jargon — testable by injecting only filings + news.
open_questions: How does BloombergGPT-50B compare to a 70B+ general open base with no finance tuning on the same evals? Public anecdotal evidence says the stronger general base wins on most; controlled comparison is missing.
